title: sideritis lurida var. relegata
intro: Sideritis lurida var. relegata, also known as Greek mountain tea or mountain tea, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of Greece. It is a member of the mint family and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and menstrual cramps, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and boosting the immune system. Sideritis lurida var. relegata is often prepared as a herbal tea, infused in hot water to release its active compounds and is also available in dried form, allowing for the creation of various herbal preparations, such as capsules and tinctures.
